title: What is Multi-Object Tracking?
concept: Multi-Object Tracking (MOT) follows multiple objects across video frames, assigning each a consistent identity over time — not just detecting where objects are, but who is who as they move.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the next logical vision application after Super-Resolution?

options.text: Multi-Object Tracking, following detected objects consistently across video frames
Restarting the curriculum from logic gates

exercises.explain: Yes! We move from sharpening single frames to tracking objects across many frames.

cards.title: Who is who across the crowd?
cards.body: A detector can find 10 people in a single video frame. But in the next frame, how do you know which of those 10 boxes is the SAME person as before, versus a different person who just walked into view?

cards.title: Multi-Object Tracking
cards.body: Multi-Object Tracking (MOT) follows multiple objects across a video sequence, assigning each one a consistent track ID that persists frame to frame, even as objects move, overlap, or briefly disappear.

cards.title: Name tags at a moving party
cards.body: Detection is like taking a photo of a party and counting how many people are in it. Tracking is like giving each guest a name tag, then following those SAME name tags as everyone mingles and moves around the room across many photos.

cards.title: Traffic monitoring
cards.body: A traffic camera detects 5 cars in frame 1. In frame 2, those same 5 cars have moved slightly. MOT keeps 'Car #3' labeled as Car #3 across every frame, letting the system compute each car's speed and trajectory over time.

cards.title: Tracking isn't just detection repeated every frame
cards.body: It's tempting to think tracking is just running your detector on every frame. But running detection alone gives you fresh, unlabeled boxes each frame with no memory of identity — you'd have no way to know Car #3 in frame 10 is the same car as Car #3 in frame 1.

left.text:
Finds objects in ONE frame
No memory across frames
Answers: 'What's here right now?'

right.text:
Follows objects ACROSS frames
Maintains consistent identity
Answers: 'Who is who over time?'

exercises.prompt: What does Multi-Object Tracking add on top of plain object detection?

options.text: A consistent identity (track ID) for each object that persists across video frames
Higher image resolution for each frame

exercises.explain: Correct! Tracking maintains consistent identity over time, unlike single-frame detection.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Running an object detector independently on every frame automatically gives you consistent object identities.
exercises.explain: False. Independent per-frame detection gives fresh, unlabeled boxes with no memory of which object is which across frames.

exercises.sentence: Multi-Object Tracking assigns each object a persistent track ___ across frames.
exercises.explain: A consistent track ID lets you follow the same object over time.

exercises.prompt: Why can't traffic speed be computed from single-frame detection alone?

options.text: Speed requires knowing the SAME car's position across multiple frames, which requires tracking, not just detection
Speed can be computed directly from one still image
Detection models cannot process video at all

exercises.explain: Correct! Speed needs identity continuity across frames, which is exactly what tracking provides.

exercises.prompt: A security system detects 3 people in every frame of a video, but frequently mislabels which detected box belongs to which person as they cross paths. What is missing?

options.text: A tracking algorithm to maintain consistent identity (track IDs) across frames, especially through occlusions and crossings
A higher resolution camera; tracking is unrelated to resolution
Nothing is missing; per-frame detection is sufficient for identity tracking

exercises.explain: Exactly! Identity continuity requires tracking logic, not just detection. Next: the standard paradigm — tracking-by-detection.
